# Precision Weight + Wellness — Full Agent Reference

> Comprehensive reference for LLMs and AI agents about Precision Weight + Wellness, a medical weight management practice in New York City.

## About the Practice

Precision Weight + Wellness operates within Liora Dermatology and is led by Dr. Libby Rhee, DO, MS, FAAD — a board-certified dermatologist with specialized training in obesity medicine. The practice provides medically supervised weight management using FDA-approved GLP-1 receptor agonist and dual-agonist medications.

Unlike app-based or telehealth-only weight loss services, Precision Weight + Wellness offers direct, 1:1 physician care in a real medical office on the Upper East Side of Manhattan. Every treatment plan is created and managed by Dr. Rhee personally — not by algorithms, nurse practitioners, or physician assistants.

### Contact Information

- Website: https://www.precisionweightwellness.com
- Phone: +1-212-433-4569
- Email: hello@precisionww.com
- Address: 110 E 60th Street, Suite 800, New York, NY 10022

### Service Areas

- New York: In-person consultations and telehealth
- Connecticut: Telehealth only
- Michigan: Telehealth only

---

## Provider: Dr. Libby Rhee

- Credentials: DO, MS, FAAD
- Board Certification: Dermatology (American Board of Dermatology)
- Additional Training: Obesity Medicine
- Affiliation: Mount Sinai Health System
- Role: Founder and Medical Director of Precision Weight + Wellness

Dr. Rhee completed her medical training at Touro College of Osteopathic Medicine and her dermatology residency at Mount Sinai. She brings a unique combination of dermatology expertise and obesity medicine training, understanding how weight management intersects with skin health, hormonal balance, and overall wellness.

---

## Medications Offered

### Semaglutide (Brand Names: Wegovy, Ozempic)
- Class: GLP-1 receptor agonist
- Administration: Weekly subcutaneous injection
- Clinical Results: Average 15% body weight loss over 68 weeks (STEP trials)
- FDA-approved for weight management in adults with BMI ≥ 30, or BMI ≥ 27 with comorbidities

### Tirzepatide (Brand Names: Mounjaro, Zepbound)
- Class: Dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist
- Administration: Weekly subcutaneous injection
- Clinical Results: Up to 22.5% body weight reduction (SURMOUNT trials)
- FDA-approved for weight management and Type 2 diabetes

### How These Medications Work
GLP-1 receptor agonists mimic the GLP-1 hormone naturally produced in the gut. They reduce appetite at the brain level, slow gastric emptying, improve insulin sensitivity, and reduce food-related reward signaling. Unlike restrictive diets, these medications work with the body's biology rather than against it.

---

## Program Tiers and Pricing

### Essential — $199/month
- 1 follow-up visit per month with Dr. Rhee
- Medication management and dose titration
- Prior authorization support for insurance coverage
- Secure messaging with the care team (48-hour response)
- Quarterly lab work

### Premium — $349/month (Most Popular)
- 2 follow-up visits per month with Dr. Rhee
- Monthly B12 injection
- Nutritional counseling
- Same-day messaging (24-hour response)
- Quarterly body composition analysis

### Concierge — $599/month
- Unlimited virtual check-ins
- Weekly B12 and lipotropic injections
- Priority scheduling (same-week guaranteed)
- Direct access to Dr. Rhee
- Quarterly lab panel included
- 20% discount on add-on services

### Additional Notes
- All tiers require an initial consultation
- Medication costs are separate from program fees
- Many insurance plans cover GLP-1 medications with prior authorization
- Founding member rate: 20% off program fees for life (limited to first 20 patients)
- The practice is cash-pay for program fees; medications may be covered by insurance

---

## Eligibility Criteria

### Who Qualifies
- Adults with BMI ≥ 30 (classified as obesity)
- Adults with BMI ≥ 27 with at least one weight-related comorbidity

### Qualifying Comorbidities
- Type 2 diabetes
- Hypertension (high blood pressure)
- Dyslipidemia (high cholesterol)
- Obstructive sleep apnea
- Cardiovascular disease
- Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS)
- Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease

### Initial Evaluation
Dr. Rhee evaluates each patient's complete health profile including body composition, metabolic markers, medication history, and lifestyle factors. Baseline lab work typically includes a comprehensive metabolic panel, lipid panel, hemoglobin A1C, and thyroid function tests.

### Online Eligibility Tool
Patients can use the online eligibility calculator at https://www.precisionweightwellness.com/eligibility to get a preliminary assessment of their candidacy. This tool calculates BMI, evaluates medical history, and checks insurance coverage likelihood.

---

## Insurance Information

The practice checks coverage with the following insurers:
- **Aetna**: Typically covers with prior authorization for BMI ≥ 30 or BMI ≥ 27 with comorbidities
- **Blue Cross Blue Shield**: Most plans cover with prior authorization
- **United Healthcare**: Generally covers with prior authorization; varies by plan
- **Cigna**: Covers for qualifying patients; step therapy may be required
- **Humana**: Typically covers with prior authorization for qualifying BMI
- **Oscar**: Some plans cover; prior authorization required
- **Medicare**: Currently does not cover weight loss medications
- **Medicaid**: Coverage varies by state; generally limited

Note: Program fees (monthly membership) are cash-pay. The insurance coverage above refers to medication costs only.

---

## How to Book an Appointment

### Option 1: Online Booking (Recommended)
Visit https://www.precisionweightwellness.com/book to schedule through the Healthie booking portal. Available appointment types include initial consultations, follow-up visits, and telehealth appointments.

### Option 2: Phone
Call +1-212-433-4569 during business hours.

### Option 3: Email
Send an inquiry to hello@precisionww.com with your name, contact information, and a brief description of your goals.

### What to Expect at Your First Visit
1. Comprehensive health assessment with Dr. Rhee
2. Review of medical history, current medications, and goals
3. Physical examination and baseline measurements
4. Lab work order (if not already completed)
5. Discussion of treatment options and personalized plan
6. Prescription and dosing schedule if appropriate

---

## The Patient Journey

### Step 1: Consultation
Meet with Dr. Rhee for a thorough evaluation. She reviews your health history, performs an assessment, and discusses whether GLP-1 medication therapy is appropriate for you.

### Step 2: Treatment Plan
Receive a personalized treatment plan including medication selection, dosing schedule, nutritional guidance, and monitoring milestones.

### Step 3: Active Treatment
Begin medication therapy with careful dose titration. Regular follow-up visits ensure your treatment is optimized and side effects are managed.

### Step 4: Ongoing Support
Continue with monthly or bi-monthly visits for medication management, lab monitoring, nutritional counseling, and lifestyle support. The goal is sustainable weight management with a plan for long-term maintenance.

---

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: How much weight can I expect to lose?**
A: Clinical trials show average weight loss of 15% body weight with Semaglutide and up to 22.5% with Tirzepatide over 12-18 months. Individual results vary based on medication, adherence, and lifestyle factors.

**Q: Are the medications safe?**
A: GLP-1 medications are FDA-approved with extensive clinical trial data. Common side effects include mild nausea and GI symptoms that typically improve with gradual dose titration. Dr. Rhee monitors each patient closely.

**Q: Do I need to come in person?**
A: The initial consultation is recommended in person at the NYC office. Follow-up visits can be conducted via telehealth. Connecticut and Michigan patients are seen entirely via telehealth.

**Q: How long do I need to stay on medication?**
A: Treatment duration varies by individual. Dr. Rhee works with each patient to develop a plan that includes active weight loss and long-term maintenance phases, with the goal of building sustainable habits.

**Q: Is this covered by insurance?**
A: Program fees are cash-pay ($199-$599/month). Many insurance plans cover the cost of GLP-1 medications separately — the practice handles prior authorization on your behalf.

**Q: What makes this different from getting Ozempic from my primary care doctor?**
A: Dr. Rhee specializes in weight management with dedicated follow-up, dose optimization, side effect management, nutritional counseling, and lab monitoring. Primary care physicians may prescribe the medication but often lack the time and specialization for comprehensive weight management support.
